scheme_name: Transgender Utthan Kosh - Training Programme for Transgender
page_title: Transgender Utthan Kosh - Training Programme for Transgender

details: The scheme "Transgender Utthan Kosh - Training Programme for Transgenders" was launched by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Rajasthan, to provide financial assistance, skill training, and employment opportunities to transgender individuals, promoting their inclusion in society.

Under this scheme, vocational and technical training is provided to transgender persons through Rajasthan Skill and Livelihood Development Corporation (RSLDC), Rajasthan Knowledge Corporation Limited (RKCL), Jan Shikshan Sansthan, Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs), or other training providers recognized by the State or Central Government. Transgender individuals can avail a maximum of two training programs under this scheme. The Social Justice and Empowerment Department implements the scheme in collaboration with recognized training institutes and organizations.
benefits: Vocational/technical training will be provided to transgender persons through the Rajasthan Skill and Livelihood Development Corporation (RSLDC), Rajasthan Knowledge Corporation Limited (RKCL), Jan Shikshan Sansthan, Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs), or training providers recognized by the State or Central Government.
After receiving skill development training, each trainee will be given a lump sum amount of ₹5,000/- per month for one course (for maximum three months and for two courses).
Transgender persons can avail maximum 2 training programmes only.
The state government reimburses the training fee directly to the training institutions.
Upon completion of training, the beneficiary will receive a certificate and employment assistance.
eligibility: The applicant should be a resident of Rajasthan.
The applicant should be a transgender person.
The gross annual income of applicant should not exceed ₹8,00,000/-.
A maximum of two training courses can be availed under this scheme.
The applicant must meet the minimum age and educational qualification criteria set by the respective training institution.
The State Government will reimburse the prescribed fees and other expenses to the training providing institute for the concerned programme.
The fees will be made available directly to the concerned training institute after the transgender person is admitted to the respective programme.

offline: The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the Social Justice and Empowerment Office and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Social Justice and Empowerment Office.
Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the Social Justice and Empowerment Office to whom the application has been submitted.

District Officer, Social Justice and Empowerment Department will scrutinize the received application within a maximum period of 1 month and issue administrative and financial approval and make the payment.
The training fee will be reimbursed directly to the training institute upon successful enrollment.
The transgender person shall apply for training through the identified vocational / technical training programmes conducted through the concerned institute / training providers.

documents_required: Original Residence Certificate/Ration Card/Voter ID Card

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: A transgender individual residing in Rajasthan and meeting the training criteria can apply.

faqs.question: What kind of training programs are covered?
faqs.answer: Vocational and technical training programs recognized by state or central government institutions.

faqs.question: How much financial assistance is provided?
faqs.answer: Up to ₹5,000 per training course, for a maximum of two courses.

faqs.question: Is there any age limit to apply?
faqs.answer: The applicant must meet the minimum age criteria set by the training institute.

faqs.question: Where can I get vocational training?
faqs.answer: Institutions like RSLDC, RKCL, ITIs, or any government-recognized training centers.

faqs.question: How will the training fees be paid?
faqs.answer: The government directly reimburses the training institution.

faqs.question: Can transgender individuals from other states apply?
faqs.answer: No, only Rajasthan residents are eligible.

faqs.question: How long does the approval process take?
faqs.answer: The district officer processes applications within one month.

faqs.question: Is the financial assistance directly transferred to the beneficiary?
faqs.answer: No, it is transferred to the training institution.

faqs.question: Can I apply for multiple training programs?
faqs.answer: Yes, up to two training courses are allowed.
